rules.name: ClassDependencyTreeRule
rules.message: Dependency tree complexity %d is over %d. Refactor __construct() dependencies or split up.
rules.description: Find classes with complex constructor dependency tree = current class complexity + complexity of all __construct() dependencies.

rules.name: ClassLikeCognitiveComplexityRule
rules.message: Class cognitive complexity is %d, keep it under %d

rules.name: FunctionLikeCognitiveComplexityRule
rules.message: Cognitive complexity for "%s" is %d, keep it under %d
rules.description: Based on https://www.sonarsource.com/docs/CognitiveComplexity.pdf

rules.name: DoctrineKeyValueStyleRule
rules.message: Argument #0 expects a constant string, got

rules.name: QueryPlanAnalyzerRule
rules.message: Query is not using an index on table '%s'.

rules.name: SyntaxErrorInDibiPreparedStatementMethodRule
rules.message: fetchPairs requires exactly 2 selected columns, got

rules.name: DeadCodeRule
rules.message: {$mainErrorMessage}{$exclusionMessage}

rules.name: BreakControlStructure
rules.description: Reports on using the break control structure.

rules.name: ClassConstantUsages
rules.description: Reports on class constant usage.

rules.name: ConstantUsages
rules.description: Reports on constant usage.

rules.name: ContinueControlStructure
rules.description: Reports on using the continue control structure.

rules.name: DeclareControlStructure
rules.description: Reports on using the declare control structure.

rules.name: DoWhileControlStructure
rules.description: Reports on using the do-while loop.

rules.name: EchoCalls
rules.description: Reports on dynamically calling echo().

rules.name: ElseControlStructure
rules.description: Reports on using the else control structure.

rules.name: ElseIfControlStructure
rules.description: Reports on using the elseif control structure. But not the "else if" as that's parsed as "else" followed by "if".

rules.name: EmptyCalls
rules.description: Reports on dynamically calling empty().

rules.name: EvalCalls
rules.description: Reports on dynamically calling eval().

rules.name: ExitDieCalls
rules.description: Reports on dynamically calling exit() & die().

rules.name: ForControlStructure
rules.description: Reports on using the for loop.

rules.name: ForeachControlStructure
rules.description: Reports on using the foreach loop.

rules.name: FunctionCalls
rules.description: Reports on dynamically calling a disallowed function.

rules.name: FunctionFirstClassCallables
rules.description: Reports on first class callable syntax for a disallowed method.

rules.name: GlobalKeyword
rules.description: Reports on using the global keyword.

rules.name: GotoControlStructure
rules.description: Reports on using the goto control structure.

rules.name: IfControlStructure
rules.description: Reports on using the if control structure.

rules.name: InstancePropertyUsages
rules.description: Reports on an instance property usage.

rules.name: IssetCalls
rules.description: Reports on dynamically calling isset().

rules.name: MatchControlStructure
rules.description: Reports on using the match control structure.

rules.name: MethodCalls
rules.description: Reports on dynamically calling a disallowed method or two.

rules.name: MethodFirstClassCallables
rules.description: Reports on first class callable syntax for a disallowed method.

rules.name: NewCalls
rules.description: Reports on creating objects (calling constructors).

rules.name: PrintCalls
rules.description: Reports on dynamically calling print().

rules.name: RequireIncludeControlStructure
rules.description: Reports on using the foreach loop.

rules.name: ReturnControlStructure
rules.description: Reports on using the return control structure.

rules.name: ShellExecCalls
rules.description: Reports on dynamically using the execution backtick operator (`ls`).

rules.name: StaticCalls
rules.description: Reports on statically calling a disallowed method or two.

rules.name: StaticFirstClassCallables
rules.description: Reports on first class callable syntax for a disallowed static method.

rules.name: StaticPropertyUsages
rules.description: Reports on a static property usage.

rules.name: SwitchControlStructure
rules.description: Reports on using the switch control structure.

rules.name: UnsetCalls
rules.description: Reports on dynamically calling unset().

rules.name: VariableUsages
rules.description: Reports on a variable name usage.

rules.name: WhileControlStructure
rules.description: Reports on using the while loop.

rules.name: DeclareStrictTypesRule
rules.message: File is missing a "declare(strict_types=1)" declaration.
rules.description: This rule reports an error when a non-empty file does not contain a declare(strict_types=1) declaration.

rules.name: FinalInAbstractClassRule
rules.description: This rule reports an error when a concrete public or protected method in an abstract class is not final.

rules.name: FinalRule
rules.description: This rule reports an error when a non-anonymous class is not final.

rules.name: InvokeParentHookMethodRule
rules.description: This rule reports an error when a hook method that overrides a hook method in a parent class does not invoke the overridden hook method in the expected order.

rules.name: NoAssignByReferenceRule
rules.message: Assign by reference should not be used.
rules.description: This rule reports an error when a variable is assigned by reference.

rules.name: NoCompactRule
rules.message: Function compact() should not be used.
rules.description: This rule reports an error when the function compact() is used.

rules.name: NoConstructorParameterWithDefaultValueRule
rules.description: This rule reports an error when a constructor declared in

rules.name: NoErrorSuppressionRule
rules.message: Error suppression via "@" should not be used.
rules.description: This rule reports an error when @ is used to suppress errors.

rules.name: NoEvalRule
rules.message: Language construct eval() should not be used.
rules.description: This rule reports an error when the language construct eval() is used.

rules.name: NoExtendsRule
rules.description: This rule reports an error when a class extends another class.

rules.name: NoIssetRule
rules.message: Language construct isset() should not be used.
rules.description: This rule reports an error when the language construct isset() is used.

rules.name: NoNamedArgumentRule
rules.description: This rule reports an error when an anonymous function, a function, or a method is invoked using a named argument.

rules.name: NoNullableReturnTypeDeclarationRule
rules.message: Closure has a nullable return type declaration.
rules.description: This rule reports an error when a method declared in

rules.name: NoNullableReturnTypeDeclarationRule
rules.description: This rule reports an error when a method declared in

rules.name: NoNullableReturnTypeDeclarationRule
rules.description: This rule reports an error when a method declared in

rules.name: NoParameterPassedByReferenceRule
rules.description: This rule reports an error when a method has a parameter that is passed by reference.

rules.name: NoParameterPassedByReferenceRule
rules.description: This rule reports an error when a method has a parameter that is passed by reference.

rules.name: NoParameterPassedByReferenceRule
rules.description: This rule reports an error when a method has a parameter that is passed by reference.

rules.name: NoParameterWithContainerTypeDeclarationRule
rules.description: This rule reports an error when a method has a type declaration for a known dependency injection container or service locator.

rules.name: NoParameterWithNullDefaultValueRule
rules.description: This rule reports an error when a method declared in

rules.name: NoParameterWithNullDefaultValueRule
rules.description: This rule reports an error when a method declared in

rules.name: NoParameterWithNullDefaultValueRule
rules.description: This rule reports an error when a method declared in

rules.name: NoParameterWithNullableTypeDeclarationRule
rules.description: This rule reports an error when a method declared in

rules.name: NoParameterWithNullableTypeDeclarationRule
rules.description: This rule reports an error when a method declared in

rules.name: NoParameterWithNullableTypeDeclarationRule
rules.description: This rule reports an error when a method declared in

rules.name: NoPhpstanIgnoreRule
rules.description: This rule reports an error when a @phpstan-ignore, @phpstan-ignore-line, or @phpstan-ignore-next-line tag is used to ignore errors reported by phpstan/phpstan.

rules.name: NoReturnByReferenceRule
rules.description: This rule reports an error when a method returns by reference.

rules.name: NoReturnByReferenceRule
rules.description: This rule reports an error when a method returns by reference.

rules.name: NoSwitchRule
rules.message: Control structures using switch should not be used.
rules.description: This rule reports an error when the statement switch() is used.

rules.name: PrivateInFinalClassRule
rules.description: This rule reports an error when a method in a final class is protected but could be private.

rules.name: TestCaseWithSuffixRule
rules.description: This rule reports an error when a concrete class is a sub-class of PHPUnit\Framework\TestCase but does not have a Test suffix.

rules.name: ConfigCollectionRule
rules.message: Config key \

rules.name: DeferrableServiceProviderMissingProvidesRule
rules.message: ServiceProviders that implement the "DeferrableProvider" interface should implement the "provides" method that returns an array of strings or class-strings

rules.name: ModelAppendsRule
rules.message: Property '%s' is not a computed property, remove from \$appends.
rules.description: This rule validates that properties in the $appends array both exist in the model and are computed properties.

rules.name: NoEnvCallsOutsideOfConfigRule
rules.message: Called 'env' outside of the config directory which returns null when the config is cached, use 'config'.
rules.description: Catches `env()` calls outside of the config directory.

rules.name: NoMissingTranslationsRule
rules.message: Translation "

rules.name: NoModelMakeRule
rules.message: Called 'Model::make()' which performs unnecessary work, use 'new Model()'.
rules.description: Catches inefficient instantiation of models using Model::make().

rules.name: NoPublicModelScopeAndAccessorRule
rules.message: Local query scope method '%s' should be declared as protected.

rules.name: NoUnnecessaryCollectionCallRule
rules.message: Called '%s' on Laravel collection, but could have been retrieved as a query.
rules.description: This rule checks for unnecessary heavy operations on the Collection class that could have instead been performed on the Builder class.

rules.name: NoUnnecessaryEnumerableToArrayCallsRule
rules.message: Called [toArray()] on an Enumerable which does not contain any Arrayables.
rules.description: This rule checks for unnecessary calls `Enumerable::toArray()` that could have used `all()` instead. The `toArray()` method recursively converts all Arrayable items in the Enumerable to an array and if none of the items are Arrayable, it is unnecessary map call.
rules.tip: Use [all()] to get the items as an array.

rules.name: NoUselessValueFunctionCallsRule
rules.message: Calling the helper function 'value()' without a closure as the first argument simply returns the first argument without doing anything

rules.name: NoUselessWithFunctionCallsRule
rules.message: Calling the helper function 'with()' with only one argument simply returns the value itself. If you want to chain methods on a construct, use '(new ClassName())->foo()' instead

rules.name: OctaneCompatibilityRule
rules.message: Consider using bind method instead or pass a closure.

rules.name: RelationExistenceRule
rules.message: Relation '%s' is not found in %s model.

rules.name: UndefinedArgumentOrOptionRule
rules.message: Command "%s" does not have argument "%s".

rules.name: UnusedViewsRule
rules.message: This view is not used in the project.

rules.name: AllowComparingOnlyComparableTypesRule
rules.message: Comparison {$leftTypeDescribed} {$node->getOperatorSigil()} {$rightTypeDescribed} contains non-comparable type, only int|float|string|DateTimeInterface or comparable tuple is allowed.
rules.description: - Denies using comparison operators >,<,<=,>=,<=> over anything other than int|string|float|DateTimeInterface|BcMath\Number or same size tuples containing comparable types. Null is not allowed. - Mixing different types in those operators is also forbidden, only exception is comparing floats with integers and integers with BcMath\Number - Mainly targets to accidental comparisons of objects, enums or arrays which is valid in PHP, but very tricky

rules.name: BackedEnumGenericsRule
rules.message: Class {$classReflection->getName()} extends generic BackedEnum, but does not specify its type. Use @implements $expectedTag
rules.description: - Ensures that every BackedEnum child defines generic type - This rule makes sense only when BackedEnum was hacked to be generic by stub as described in this article - This rule does nothing if BackedEnum is not set to be generic, which is a default setup. Use following config to really start using it:

rules.name: ClassSuffixNamingRule
rules.message: Class name $className should end with $suffix suffix
rules.description: - Allows you to enforce class name suffix for subclasses of configured superclass - Checks nothing by default, configure it by passing superclass => suffix mapping - Passed superclass is not expected to have such suffix, only subclasses are - You can use interface as superclass

rules.name: EnforceClosureParamNativeTypehintRule
rules.message: Missing parameter typehint for {$type} parameter \${$param->var->name}.
rules.description: - Enforces usage of native typehints for closure & arrow function parameters - Does nothing on PHP 7.4 and below as native mixed is not available there - Can be configured by allowMissingTypeWhenInferred: true to allow missing typehint when it can be inferred from the context

rules.name: EnforceEnumMatchRule
rules.message: This condition contains always-$trueFalse enum comparison of $enumCases[0]. Use match expression instead, PHPStan will report unhandled enum cases
rules.description: - Enforces usage of match ($enum) instead of exhaustive conditions like if ($enum === Enum::One) elseif ($enum === Enum::Two) - This rule aims to "fix" a bit problematic behaviour of PHPStan (introduced at 1.10.0 and fixed in 1.10.34). It understands enum cases very well and forces you to adjust following code:

rules.name: EnforceIteratorToArrayPreserveKeysRule
rules.message: Calling iterator_to_array without 2nd parameter $preserve_keys. Default value true might cause failures or data loss.
rules.description: - Enforces presence of second parameter in iterator_to_array call ($preserve_keys) as the default value true is generally dangerous (risk of data loss / failure) - You can use both true and false there, but doing so is intentional choice now

rules.name: EnforceListReturnRule
rules.message: {$callLikeType} {$methodReflection->getName()} always return list, but is marked as {$returnTypeString}
rules.description: - Enforces usage of list<T> when list is always returned from a class method or function - When only single return with empty array is present in the method, it is not considered as list - Does nothing when list types are disabled in PHPStan - Consider enabling also reportAnyTypeWideningInVarTag in native PHPStan as it mostly affects lists

rules.name: EnforceNativeReturnTypehintRule
rules.message: Missing native return typehint %s
rules.description: - Enforces usage of native return typehints if supported by your PHP version - If PHPDoc is present, it deduces needed typehint from that, if not, deduction is performed based on real types returned - Applies to class methods, closures and functions - Is disabled, if you have PHPStan set up with treatPhpDocTypesAsCertain: false - Limitations: - Does not suggest parent typehint - Ignores trait methods

rules.name: EnforceReadonlyPublicPropertyRule
rules.message: Public property `{$node->getName()}` not marked as readonly.
rules.description: - Ensures immutability of all public properties by enforcing readonly modifier - No modifier needed for readonly classes in PHP 8.2 - Does nothing if PHP version does not support readonly properties (PHP 8.0 and below) - Can be configured to exclude properties with a default value

rules.name: ForbidArithmeticOperationOnNonNumberRule
rules.description: - Disallows using arithmetic operators with non-numeric types (only float, int and BcMath\Number is allowed) - You can allow numeric-string by using allowNumericString: true configuration - Modulo operator (%) allows only integers as it emits deprecation otherwise - Plus operator is allowed for merging arrays - float and BcMath\Number cannot be combined as it emits deprecations

rules.name: ForbidCastRule
rules.message: Using $castString is discouraged, please avoid using that.
rules.description: - Deny casting you configure - Possible values to use: - (array) - denied by default - (object) - denied by default - (unset) - denied by default - (bool) - (int) - (string) - (float) - forbids using also (double) and (real)
rules.wrong_code:
$empty = (array) null; // denied cast
$notEmpty = (array) 0; // denied cast

rules.name: ForbidCheckedExceptionInCallableRule
rules.message: Throwing checked exception $exceptionClass in $where!
rules.description: - Denies throwing checked exception in callables (Closures, Arrow functions and First class callables) as those cannot be tracked as checked by PHPStan analysis, because it is unknown when the callable is about to be called - It is allowed to throw checked exceptions in immediately called callables (e.g. params marked by @param-immediately-invoked-callable, see docs) - It allows configuration of functions/methods, where the callable is handling all thrown exceptions and it is safe to throw anything from there; this basically makes such calls ignored by this rule - It ignores implicitly thrown Throwable - Learn more in 🇨🇿 talk about checked exceptions in general (🇺🇸 slides)
rules.tip: If this callable is immediately called within '$usedAsArgumentOfMethodName', you should add @param-immediately-invoked-callable there. Then this error disappears and the exception will be properly propagated.

rules.name: ForbidCheckedExceptionInYieldingMethodRule
rules.message: Throwing checked exception $exceptionClass in yielding $functionName is denied as it gets thrown upon Generator iteration
rules.description: - Denies throwing checked exception within yielding methods as those exceptions are not throw upon method call, but when generator gets iterated. - This behaviour cannot be easily reflected within PHPStan exception analysis and may cause false negatives. - Make sure you have enabled checked exceptions, otherwise, this rule does nothing

rules.name: ForbidCustomFunctionsRule
rules.description: - Allows you to easily deny some approaches within your codebase by denying classes, methods and functions - Configuration syntax is array where key is method name and value is reason used in error message - Works even with interfaces, constructors and some dynamic class/method names like $fn = 'sleep'; $fn();
rules.wrong_code:
new SomeClass(); // Class SomeClass is forbidden. Please use different class
(new AnotherClass())->someMethod(); // Method AnotherClass::someMethod() is forbidden. Please use anotherMethod

rules.name: ForbidEnumInFunctionArgumentsRule
rules.message: Argument{$plural} {$wrongArgumentsString} in {$node->name->toString()}() cannot contain enum {$reason}
rules.description: - Guards passing native enums to native functions where it fails / produces warning or does unexpected behaviour - Most of the array manipulation functions does not work with enums as they do implicit __toString conversion inside, but that is not possible to do with enums - See test for all functions and their problems

rules.name: ForbidFetchOnMixedRule
rules.description: - Denies constant/property fetch on unknown type. - Any property fetch assumes the caller is an object with such property and therefore, the typehint/phpdoc should be fixed. - Similar to forbidMethodCallOnMixed - Makes sense only on PHPStan level 8 or below, gets autodisabled on level 9

rules.name: ForbidIdenticalClassComparisonRule
rules.message: Using {$node->getOperatorSigil()} with {$forbiddenObjectType->describe(VerbosityLevel::typeOnly())} is denied
rules.description: - Denies comparing configured classes by === or !== - Default configuration contains only DateTimeInterface - You may want to add more classes from your codebase or vendor

rules.name: ForbidIncrementDecrementOnNonIntegerRule
rules.description: - Denies using $i++, $i--, ++$i, --$i with any non-integer - PHP itself is leading towards stricter behaviour here and soft-deprecated **some** non-integer usages in 8.3, see RFC
rules.wrong_code:
$value = '2e0';
$value++; // would be float(3), denied

rules.name: ForbidMatchDefaultArmForEnumsRule
rules.message: Default arm is denied for enums in match, list all values so that this case is raised when new enum case is added.
rules.description: - Denies using default arm in match() construct when native enum is passed as subject - This rules makes sense only as a complement of native phpstan rule that guards that all enum cases are handled in match arms - As a result, you are forced to add new arm when new enum case is added. That brings up all the places in your codebase that needs new handling.

rules.name: ForbidMethodCallOnMixedRule
rules.description: - Denies calling methods on unknown type. - Any method call assumes the caller is an object with such method and therefore, the typehint/phpdoc should be fixed. - Similar to forbidFetchOnMixed - Makes sense only on PHPStan level 8 or below, gets autodisabled on level 9

rules.name: ForbidNotNormalizedTypeRule
rules.message: Found non-normalized type {$multiTypeNodeString} for {$identification}: {$typeNodeBString} is a subtype of {$typeNodeAString}.
rules.description: - Reports PhpDoc or native type that is not normalized, which can be: - when child and parent appears in its union or intersection - when same type appears multiple times in its union or intersection - when DNF is not used - configurable by checkDisjunctiveNormalForm - supports: - parameter typehints & @param phpdoc - return typehint & @return phpdoc - property typehint & @var phpdoc - inline @var phpdoc - @throws phpdoc - multi-catch statements - Main motivation here is that PHPStan normalizes all types before analysis, so it is better to see it in codebase the same way PHPStan does

rules.name: ForbidNullInAssignOperationsRule
rules.message: Null value involved in {$operator} assignment on the right side.
rules.description: - Denies using assign operators if null is involved on right side - You can configure which operators are ignored, by default only ??= is excluded

rules.name: ForbidNullInBinaryOperationsRule
rules.message: Null value involved in binary operation: {$leftTypeDescribed} {$node->getOperatorSigil()} {$rightTypeDescribed}
rules.description: - Denies using binary operators if null is involved on either side - You can configure which operators are ignored. Default ignore is excluding only ===, !==, ?? - Following custom setup is recommended when using latest phpstan-strict-rules and allowComparingOnlyComparableTypes is enabled

rules.name: ForbidNullInInterpolatedStringRule
rules.message: Null value involved in string interpolation with
rules.description: - Disallows using nullable expressions within double-quoted strings - This should probably comply with setup of concat operator (.) in forbidNullInBinaryOperations so if you blacklisted it there, you might want to disable this rule

rules.name: ForbidPhpDocNullabilityMismatchWithNativeTypehintRule
rules.message: The $phpDocIdentification phpdoc does not contain null, but native return type does
rules.description: - Disallows having nullable native typehint while using non-nullable phpdoc - Checks @return and @param over methods and @var over properties - PHPStan itself allows using subtype of native type in phpdoc, but resolves overall type as union of those types making such phpdoc actually invalid
rules.wrong_code:
/**
 * @param string $param
 */
public function sayHello(?string $param) {} // invalid phpdoc not containing null

rules.name: ForbidProtectedEnumMethodRule
rules.message: Protected methods within enum makes no sense as you cannot extend them anyway.
rules.description: - Disallows protected method within enums as those are not extendable anyway - Ignore method declared in traits as those might be reused in regular classes

rules.name: ForbidReturnInConstructorRule
rules.message: Using return statement in constructor is forbidden to be able to check useless default values. Either create static constructors of use if-else.

rules.name: ForbidReturnValueInYieldingMethodRule
rules.message: Returned value from yielding $callType can be accessed only via Generator::getReturn, $suffix.
rules.description: - Disallows returning values in yielding methods unless marked to return Generator as the value is accessible only via Generator::getReturn - To prevent misuse, this rule can be configured to even stricter mode where it reports such returns regardless of return type declared

rules.name: ForbidUnsafeArrayKeyRule
rules.message: Array key must be integer or string, but
rules.description: - Denies non-int non-string array keys - PHP casts null, float and bool to some nearest int/string - You should rather do that intentionally and explicitly - Those types are the main difference to default PHPStan behaviour which allows using them as array keys - You can exclude reporting mixed keys via reportMixed configuration - You can exclude reporting isset($array[$invalid]) and $array[$invalid] ?? null via reportInsideIsset configuration

rules.name: ForbidUnsetClassFieldRule
rules.message: Unsetting class field is forbidden as it causes un-initialization, assign null instead
rules.description: - Denies calling unset over class field as it causes un-initialization, see https://3v4l.org/V8uuP - Null assignment should be used instead

rules.name: ForbidUnusedClosureParametersRule
rules.message: {$functionType} parameter \${$parameterName} is unused
rules.description: - Reports unused parameters in closures and arrow functions - Only reports trailing unused parameters (parameters that are unused and all parameters after them are also unused)
rules.wrong_code: fn (int $key, Item $item, string $unused) => $item->ok(); // unused parameter $unused is reported, $key not

rules.name: ForbidUnusedExceptionRule
rules.message: Method {$this->printer->prettyPrintExpr($node)} returns exception that was not used in any way.
rules.description: - Reports forgotten exception throw (created or returned from function, but not used in any way)

rules.name: ForbidUnusedMatchResultRule
rules.message: Unused match result detected, possible returns:
rules.description: - Reports forgotten usage of match result - Any match with at least one arm returning a value is checked

rules.name: ForbidUselessNullableReturnRule
rules.message: Declared return type {$declaredType->describe($verbosity)} contains null, but it is never returned. Returned types: {$returnTypeUnion->describe($verbosity)}.
rules.description: - Denies marking closure/function/method return type as nullable when null is never returned - Recommended to be used together with uselessPrivatePropertyDefaultValue

rules.name: ForbidVariableTypeOverwritingRule
rules.message: Overwriting variable \$$variableName while changing its type from {$previousVariableType->describe(VerbosityLevel::precise())} to {$newVariableType->describe(VerbosityLevel::precise())}
rules.description: - Restricts variable assignment to those that does not change its type - Array append $array[] = 1; not yet supported - Null and mixed are not taken into account, advanced phpstan types like non-empty-X are trimmed before comparison - Rule allows type generalization and type narrowing (parent <-> child)

rules.name: RequirePreviousExceptionPassRule
rules.message: Exception {$exceptionName} not passed as previous to {$this->printer->prettyPrintExpr($node)}
rules.description: - Detects forgotten exception pass-as-previous when re-throwing - Checks if caught exception can be passed as argument to the call (including constructor call) in throw node inside the catch block - You may encounter false-positives in some edge-cases, where you do not want to pass exception as previous, feel free to ignore those

rules.name: UselessPrivatePropertyDefaultValueRule
rules.message: Property {$className}::{$propertyName} has useless default value (overwritten in constructor)

rules.name: AccessGlobalVariableWithinContextRule
rules.message: Class %s %s %s and uses $%s: accessing globals in this context is considered an anti-pattern

rules.name: AccessStaticPropertyWithinModelContextRule
rules.message: Class %s %s %s and uses %s::$%s: accessing a singleton in this context is considered an anti-pattern

rules.name: GotoRule
rules.message: No goto, cmon!

rules.name: MissingClosureParameterTypehintRule
rules.message: Parameter #%d $%s of anonymous function has no typehint.

rules.name: NoDateWithoutSecondArgumentRule
rules.message: Calling date() without the second parameter is forbidden, rely on a clock abstraction like lcobucci/clock

rules.name: NoRelativeDateTimeInterfaceRule
rules.message: Instantiating %s without the first argument is forbidden, rely on a clock abstraction like lcobucci/clock

rules.name: NoRelativeStrtotimeRule
rules.message: Calling strtotime() with relative datetime "%s" without the second argument is forbidden, rely on a clock abstraction like lcobucci/clock

rules.name: NoTimeRule
rules.message: Calling %s() directly is forbidden, rely on a clock abstraction like lcobucci/clock

rules.name: PhpUnitFqcnAnnotationRule
rules.message: Class %s does not exist.

rules.name: StringToClassRule
rules.message: Class %s should be written with ::class notation, string found.

rules.name: SymfonyFilesystemRule
rules.message: Function %s is unsafe to use, rely on Symfony component Filesystem::%s instead.

rules.name: SymfonyProcessRule
rules.message: Function %s is unsafe to use, rely on Symfony\Process component instead.

rules.name: UnusedVariableRule
rules.message: %s has an unused variable $%s.

rules.name: AlreadyRegisteredAutodiscoveryServiceRule
rules.message: The "%s" service is already registered via autodiscovery ->load(), no need to set it twice

rules.name: AvoidFeatureSetAttributeInRectorRule
rules.message: Instead of using Rector rule to setAttribute("%s") to be used later, create a service extending "DecoratingNodeVisitorInterface". This ensures attribute decoration and node changes are in 2 separated steps.

rules.name: CheckRequiredInterfaceInContractNamespaceRule
rules.message: Interface must be located in "Contract" or "Contracts" namespace

rules.name: ClassNameRespectsParentSuffixRule
rules.message: Class should have suffix "%s" to respect parent type

rules.name: ExplicitExpectsMockMethodRule
rules.message: PHPUnit mock method is missing explicit expects(), e.g. $this->mock->expects($this->once())->...

rules.name: FileNameMatchesExtensionRule
rules.message: The config uses "%s" extension, but the file name is "%s". Sync them to ease discovery

rules.name: ForbiddenArrayMethodCallRule
rules.message: Array method calls [$this, "method"] are not allowed. Use explicit method instead to help PhpStorm, PHPStan and Rector understand your code

rules.name: ForbiddenExtendOfNonAbstractClassRule
rules.message: Only abstract classes can be extended

rules.name: ForbiddenFuncCallRule
rules.message: Function "%s()" cannot be used/left in the code

rules.name: ForbiddenMultipleClassLikeInOneFileRule
rules.message: Multiple class/interface/trait is not allowed in single file

rules.name: ForbiddenNodeRule
rules.message: "%s" is forbidden to use

rules.name: ForbiddenStaticClassConstFetchRule
rules.message: Avoid static access of constants, as they can change value. Use interface and contract method instead

rules.name: ForeachCeptionRule
rules.message: There is %d nested foreach nested in each other. Refactor to more flat approach or to collection to avoid high complexity

rules.name: FormTypeClassNameRule
rules.message: Class extends "%s" must have "FormType" suffix to make form explicit, "%s" given

rules.name: MaximumIgnoredErrorCountRule
rules.message: Ignored error count %d in phpstan.neon surpassed maximum limit %d.\nInstead of ignoring more errors, fix them to keep your codebase fit.

rules.name: NoAbstractControllerConstructorRule
rules.message: Abstract controller should not have constructor, to avoid override by child classes. Use #[Require] or @require and autowire() method instead
rules.description: Check if abstract controller has constructor, as it should use #[Require] instead to avoid parent constructor override

rules.name: NoArrayMapWithArrayCallableRule
rules.message: Avoid using array callables in array_map(), as it cripples static analysis on used method

rules.name: NoAssertFuncCallInTestsRule
rules.message: Instead of assert() that can miss important checks, use native PHPUnit assert call

rules.name: NoBareAndSecurityIsGrantedContentsRule
rules.message: Instead of using one long "and" condition join, split into multiple standalone #[IsGranted] attributes

rules.name: NoBundleResourceConfigRule
rules.message: Avoid using configs in Bundle/Resources directory. Move them to "/config" directory instead

rules.name: NoClassLevelRouteRule
rules.message: Avoid class-level route prefixing. Use method route to keep single source of truth and focus

rules.name: NoClassReflectionStaticReflectionRule
rules.message: Instead of "new ClassReflection()" use ReflectionProvider service or "(new PHPStan\Reflection\ClassReflection(<desired_type>))" for static reflection to work

rules.name: NoConstructorAndRequiredTogetherRule
rules.message: Avoid using __construct() and @required in the same class. Pick one to keep architecture clean

rules.name: NoConstructorOverrideRule
rules.message: Possible __construct() override, this can cause missing dependencies or setup

rules.name: NoControllerMethodInjectionRule
rules.message: Instead of service "%s" action injection, use __construct() and invokable controller with __invoke() to clearly separate services and parameters

rules.name: NoDoctrineListenerWithoutContractRule
rules.message: There should be no Doctrine listeners modified in config. Implement "Document\Event\EventSubscriber" to provide events in the class itself
rules.description: Based on https://tomasvotruba.com/blog/2019/07/22/how-to-convert-listeners-to-subscribers-and-reduce-your-configs Subscribers have much better PHP support - IDE, PHPStan + Rector - than simple yaml files

rules.name: NoDocumentMockingRule
rules.message: Instead of document mocking, create object directly to get better type support

rules.name: NoDoubleConsecutiveTestMockRule
rules.message: Do not use "willReturnOnConsecutiveCalls()" and "willReturnCallback()" on the same mock. Use "willReturnCallback() only instead to make test more clear.

rules.name: NoDuplicateArgAutowireByTypeRule
rules.message: Instead of passing "%s" to arg(), remove the line and let autowiring handle it
rules.description: Spot pointless explicit services:

rules.name: NoDuplicateArgsAutowireByTypeRule
rules.message: Instead of passing "%s" to args(), remove the line and let autowiring handle it
rules.description: Spot pointless explicit services:

rules.name: NoDynamicNameRule
rules.message: Use explicit names over dynamic ones

rules.name: NoEntityMockingRule
rules.message: Instead of entity or document mocking, create object directly to get better type support
rules.description: The ORM entities and ODM documents should never be mocked, as it leads to typeless code. Use them directly instead.

rules.name: NoEntityOutsideEntityNamespaceRule
rules.message: Class with #[Entity] attribute must be located in "Entity" namespace to be loaded by Doctrine

rules.name: NoFindTaggedServiceIdsCallRule
rules.message: Instead of "$this->findTaggedServiceIds()" use more reliable registerForAutoconfiguration() and tagged iterator attribute. Those work outside any configuration and avoid missed tag errors

rules.name: NoGetDoctrineInControllerRule
rules.message: Do not use $this->getDoctrine() method in controller. Use __construct(EntityManagerInterface $entityManager) instead

rules.name: NoGetInCommandRule
rules.message: Do not use $this->get(Type::class) method in commands to get services. Use __construct(Type $type) instead

rules.name: NoGetInControllerRule
rules.message: Do not use $this->get(Type::class) method in controller to get services. Use __construct(Type $type) instead

rules.name: NoGetRepositoryOnServiceRepositoryEntityRule
rules.message: Instead of calling "->getRepository(%s::class)" service locator, inject service repository "%s" via constructor and use it directly

rules.name: NoGetRepositoryOutsideServiceRule
rules.message: Instead of getting repository from EntityManager, use constructor injection and service pattern to keep code clean

rules.name: NoGlobalConstRule
rules.message: Global constants are forbidden. Use enum-like class list instead

rules.name: NoInstanceOfStaticReflectionRule
rules.message: Instead of "instanceof/is_a()" use ReflectionProvider service or "(new ObjectType(<desired_type>))->isSuperTypeOf(<element_type>)" for static reflection to work

rules.name: NoIntegerRefactorReturnRule
rules.message: Instead of using DONT_TRAVERSE_CHILDREN* or STOP_TRAVERSAL in refactor() method, make use of attributes. Return always node, null or REMOVE_NODE. Using traverser enums might lead to unexpected results

rules.name: NoJustPropertyAssignRule
rules.message: Instead of assigning service property to a variable, use the property directly

rules.name: NoLeadingBackslashInNameRule
rules.message: Instead of "new Name(\'\\\\Foo\')" use "new FullyQualified(\'Foo\')"

rules.name: NoListenerWithoutContractRule
rules.message: There should be no listeners modified in config. Use EventSubscriberInterface contract or #[AsEventListener] attribute and native PHP instead
rules.description: Based on https://tomasvotruba.com/blog/2019/07/22/how-to-convert-listeners-to-subscribers-and-reduce-your-configs Subscribers have much better PHP support - IDE, PHPStan + Rector - than simple yaml files

rules.name: NoMissingVariableDimFetchRule
rules.message: Dim fetch assign variable is missing, create it first

rules.name: NoMockObjectAndRealObjectPropertyRule
rules.message: Instead of ambiguous mock + object mix, pick single type that is more relevant

rules.name: NoMockOnlyTestRule
rules.message: Test should have at least one non-mocked property, to test something

rules.name: NoOnlyNullReturnInRefactorRule
rules.message: The refactor() method returns always null, but it should return at least one modified node

rules.name: NoParentRepositoryRule
rules.message: Extending EntityRepository is not allowed, use constructor injection and pass entity manager instead
rules.description: Check if class extends repository class, the entity manager should be injected via constructor instead

rules.name: NoPropertyNodeAssignRule
rules.message: Avoid assigning a node to property to avoid object juggling, pass it as argument instead

rules.name: NoProtectedClassStmtRule
rules.message: Avoid protected class stmts as they yield unexpected behavior. Use clear interface contract instead

rules.name: NoReferenceRule
rules.message: Use explicit return value over magic &reference

rules.name: NoRepositoryCallInDataFixtureRule
rules.message: Refactor read-data fixtures to write-only, make use of references

rules.name: NoRequiredOutsideClassRule
rules.message: Symfony #[Require]/@required should be used only in classes to avoid misuse

rules.name: NoReturnSetterMethodRule
rules.message: Setter method cannot return anything, only set value

rules.name: NoRouteTrailingSlashPathRule
rules.message: Avoid trailing slash in route path "%s", to prevent redirects and SEO issues

rules.name: NoRoutingPrefixRule
rules.message: Avoid global route prefixing, to use single place for paths and improve static analysis

rules.name: NoServiceAutowireDuplicateRule
rules.message: Service autowire() is called as duplicate of $services->defaults()->autowire(). Remove it on the service

rules.name: NoServiceSameNameSetClassRule
rules.message: No need to duplicate service class and name. Use only "$services->set(%s::class)" instead

rules.name: NoSetClassServiceDuplicationRule
rules.message: Instead of "$services->set(%s)->class(%s)" that brings no value, use simple $services->set(%s)

rules.name: NoStringInGetSubscribedEventsRule
rules.message: Symfony getSubscribedEvents() method must contain only event class references, no strings

rules.name: NoTestMocksRule
rules.message: Mocking "%s" class is forbidden. Use direct/anonymous class instead for better static analysis

rules.name: NoValueObjectInServiceConstructorRule
rules.message: Value object "%s" cannot be passed to constructor of a service. Pass it as a method argument instead

rules.name: ParamNameToTypeConventionRule
rules.message: Parameter name "$%s" should probably have "%s" type

rules.name: PhpUpgradeDowngradeRegisteredInSetRule
rules.message: Register "%s" service to "%s" config set

rules.name: PhpUpgradeImplementsMinPhpVersionInterfaceRule
rules.message: Rule %s must implements Rector\VersionBonding\Contract\MinPhpVersionInterface

rules.name: PreferAutowireAttributeOverConfigParamRule
rules.message: Instead of parameter reference in config, add #[Autowire(param: ...)] in the "%s" class constructor

rules.name: PreferDirectIsNameRule
rules.message: Use direct $this->isName() instead of fetching NodeNameResolver service

rules.name: PreferredClassRule
rules.message: Instead of "%s" class/interface use "%s"

rules.name: PreventParentMethodVisibilityOverrideRule
rules.message: Change "%s()" method visibility to "%s" to respect parent method visibility.

rules.name: PublicStaticDataProviderRule
rules.description: PHPUnit data provider have to be public and static

rules.name: RequireAttributeNameRule
rules.message: Attribute must have all names explicitly defined

rules.name: RequireAttributeNamespaceRule
rules.message: Attribute must be located in "Attribute" namespace

rules.name: RequireExceptionNamespaceRule
rules.message: Exception must be located in "Exception" namespace

rules.name: RequireInvokableControllerRule
rules.message: Use invokable controller with __invoke() method instead of named action method

rules.name: RequireIsGrantedEnumRule
rules.message: Instead of "%s" string, use enum constant for #[IsGranted]

rules.name: RequireQueryBuilderOnRepositoryRule
rules.message: Avoid calling ->createQueryBuilder() directly on EntityManager as it requires select() + from() calls with specific values. Use $repository->createQueryBuilder() to be safe instead

rules.name: RequireRouteNameToGenerateControllerRouteRule
rules.message: To pass a controller class to generate() method, the controller must have "#[Route(name: self::class)]" above the __invoke() method
rules.description: To pass a controller class in $this->router->generate(SomeController::class), the controller must be present #[Route(name:: self::class)

rules.name: RequireServiceRepositoryParentRule
rules.message: Repository must extend "%s", "%s" or implement "%s", so it can be injected as a service

rules.name: RequireUniqueEnumConstantRule
rules.message: Enum constants "%s" are duplicated. Make them unique instead

rules.name: RequiredOnlyInAbstractRule
rules.message: #Symfony @required or #[Required] is reserved exclusively for abstract classes. For the rest of classes, use clean constructor injection

rules.name: SeeAnnotationToTestRule
rules.message: Class "%s" is missing @see annotation with test case class reference

rules.name: ServicesExcludedDirectoryMustExistRule
rules.message: Services excluded path "%s" does not exists. You can remove it

rules.name: SingleArgEventDispatchRule
rules.message: The event dispatch() method can have only 1 arg - the event object

rules.name: SingleRequiredMethodRule
rules.message: Found %d @required methods. Use only one method to avoid unexpected behavior.

rules.name: StringFileAbsolutePathExistsRule
rules.message: File "%s" could not be found. Make sure it exists

rules.name: TaggedIteratorOverRepeatedServiceCallRule
rules.message: Instead of repeated "->call(%s, ...)" calls, pass services as tagged iterator argument to the constructor

rules.name: UppercaseConstantRule
rules.message: Constant "%s" must be uppercase

rules.name: ConstantTypeCoverageRule
rules.message: Out of %d possible constant types, only %d - %.1f %% actually have it. Add more constant types to get over %s %%

rules.name: DeclareCoverageRule
rules.message: Out of %d possible declare(strict_types=1), only %d - %.1f %% actually have it. Add more declares to get over %s %%

rules.name: ParamTypeCoverageRule
rules.message: Out of %d possible param types, only %d - %.1f %% actually have it. Add more param types to get over %s %%

rules.name: PropertyTypeCoverageRule
rules.message: Out of %d possible property types, only %d - %.1f %% actually have it. Add more property types to get over %s %%

rules.name: ReturnTypeCoverageRule
rules.message: Out of %d possible return types, only %d - %.1f %% actually have it. Add more return types to get over %s %%

rules.name: LocalOnlyPublicClassMethodRule
rules.message: Public method "%s::%s()" is used only locally and should be turned protected/private

rules.name: RelativeUnusedPublicClassMethodRule
rules.message: Found %.1f %% of public methods as unused. Reduce it under %.1f %%

rules.name: UnusedPublicClassConstRule
rules.message: Public constant "%s::%s" is never used

rules.name: UnusedPublicClassMethodRule
rules.message: Public method "%s::%s()" is never used

rules.name: UnusedPublicPropertyRule
rules.message: Public property "%s::$%s" is never used

rules.name: DisallowedCallMethodOnNullRule
rules.message: Call to %s %s::%s() on NULL.
rules.description: This code is copy&pasted from [phpstan/phpstan-src] and I used it to prevent Call to a member function on null errors while I wasn't already on level 8 where all kind of "NULL" checks are already covered by default.

rules.name: ExtendedBinaryOpRule
rules.description: This rule will check "+", "*", "/", "-", ... (operators) and "." (concatenation) for compatible types.

rules.name: WrongCastRule
rules.message: Casting to %s something that\
